selected_problem_solved: The proof establishes the claimed result under the stated/explicit interpretation that there is exactly one vertex \(b\) with \(w(b)<d_T(b)\): for any finite weighted tree \(T\) whose associated integral symmetric form is positive definite and has a unique such “bad” vertex, \(T\) contains an irreducible vertex of \(L(T)\). More specifically, if \(T\) has a weight-one vertex, that vertex is irreducible; otherwise the unique bad vertex \(b\) is irreducible.
selector_skipped_reason: N_PARALLEL_RUNS=1 — single run, no selection needed

all_runs.problem_solved: The proof establishes the claimed result under the stated/explicit interpretation that there is exactly one vertex \(b\) with \(w(b)<d_T(b)\): for any finite weighted tree \(T\) whose associated integral symmetric form is positive definite and has a unique such “bad” vertex, \(T\) contains an irreducible vertex of \(L(T)\). More specifically, if \(T\) has a weight-one vertex, that vertex is irreducible; otherwise the unique bad vertex \(b\) is irreducible.
